humanities_role_explanation: Designed specifically for art-historical visual understanding of Christian iconography, using domain-specific datasets (ArtDL, ICONCLASS, Wikidata) with Iconclass codes for saint classification
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal evaluation combining artwork images of Christian saints with natural language prompts. Three test configurations: (1) classification with class labels only, (2) classification with Iconclass descriptions, and (3) few-shot learning with exemplar images
analytical_tasks_explanation: Single-label classification of Christian saints in religious paintings, requiring recognition of complex iconographic symbols and religious themes. Tasks involve zero-shot classification using semantic labels or detailed iconographic descriptions, and few-shot learning with visual exemplars

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates multiple general-purpose vision-language models (CLIP, SigLIP) and multimodal LLMs across standardized tasks

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, Gemini-2.5-Pro, Gemini-2.5-Flash, plus vision-language models CLIP and SigLIP

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs and VLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for Christian iconography classification performance

arts_humanities_data.note: Visual Arts - uses Christian religious paintings and iconographic datasets (ArtDL, ICONCLASS AI Test Set, Wikidata) with Iconclass classification codes

quantitative_metrics.note: Accuracy scores, consistency analysis across datasets using Hamming distance for duplicate detection

english_language.note: Paper written in English, benchmark uses English class labels and descriptions

summary: This paper introduces a benchmark for evaluating vision-language models and multimodal LLMs on Christian iconography classification using three datasets (ArtDL, ICONCLASS, Wikidata) with Iconclass codes. The study compares zero-shot and few-shot performance across different prompt configurations, finding that multimodal LLMs (Gemini-2.5 Pro, GPT-4o) generally outperform traditional supervised baselines.
key_findings: Gemini-2.5 Pro achieved highest performance with 90.45% accuracy on ArtDL, while GPT-4o reached 88.20%. Performance dropped significantly on Wikidata dataset where SigLIP-SO400M achieved best results at 64.86%. Enriching prompts with iconographic descriptions generally improved zero-shot performance, but few-shot learning produced mixed results with mostly lower accuracy scores.
